sourcing_tip_logistics: At 17g billable weight, this cushion qualifies for Shipping Tier 1 — the most cost-efficient bracket for Korea-to-US/EU air freight, keeping per-unit logistics under $2.50 via ePacket or K-Packet. Combined with $2.07 estimated sourcing cost and 5x+ profit multiplier, sellers targeting $14.99–$19.99 retail on Amazon US can hold 6x–7x net margin after FBA prep and shipping. Standard cosmetic handling applies (no DG surcharges), but FDA OTC compliance for SPF actives adds 2–4 weeks to US market entry timeline. Zero current Amazon US competition creates a first-mover window; lightweight format and vegan certification align perfectly with cross-border e-commerce economics.

sourcing_tip:
[Marketing Strategy]
Amazon US sits as the highest-signal entry platform for Blue Ocean export to US and Europe, leveraging K-beauty cushion category momentum and zero incumbent competition. Target TikTok and Instagram influencers with #DaisoCushion and #KbeautyHack positioning to drive discovery traffic. Viral hashtags #윤슬광쿠션 and #대혜자 confirm domestic meme-ability translates to international content creation.
[Price / Margin Strategy]
Korea retail at 5,000 KRW converts to $2.07 landed cost. Target $12.99–$14.99 Amazon US retail captures 6–7x profit multiplier, well above the 5x threshold for strong margin cushion products. Existing SEA marketplace listings at $3.38–$10.50 validate international price elasticity and confirm margin headroom.
[B2B Sourcing Strategy]
Dinto operates as an export-active startup expanding aggressively in Japan and Taiwan as a high-growth vegan brand; prioritize inquiries on exclusive distribution or regional wholesale rights for new markets via https://dinto.co.kr/contact.html. OEM production is not available; this is a branded finished-goods play only. Minimum order quantity unspecified; negotiate based on container economics and retail channel commitment.
[Customs / Compliance Strategy]
HS Code 3304.99 applies as beauty preparation for skin care not elsewhere specified. Export status Yellow triggered by Titanium Dioxide and Ethylhexyl Triazone — both classified as FDA OTC drug actives for SPF claims in the US. Required certificates: FDA MoCRA Facility Registration (US), FDA OTC Monograph Compliance (US), CPNP Registration (EU). Vegan certification from manufacturer provides additional EU market advantage.
[Logistics / Shipping Strategy]
Tier 1 shipping classification at 17g billable weight delivers minimal per-unit freight burden, allowing sub-$1.00 landed logistics cost to US via air parcel or consolidated sea freight. Bundle with other cosmetics in mixed-SKU shipments to reduce per-unit DHL/FedEx rates and hit Amazon FBA small-and-light eligibility. Liquid and powder content requires standard cosmetic packaging compliance but poses zero hazmat surcharge risk for air freight.

translated_name: Zoom by Jung Saem Mool Glow Prep Booster
viability_reason: High domestic sell-out success and platform presence in Korea contrasts with near-zero global visibility, creating strong export potential for affordable K-beauty prep booster. Daiso collaboration sold out instantly in Jan 2026 due to affordability and brand credibility.
seo_keywords: 줌바이정샘물, Zoom by Jung Saem Mool, Glow Prep Booster, Daiso Jung Saem Mool, 광 프렙 부스터, 정샘물 프렙
sourcing_tip:
[Marketing Strategy]
Target Southeast Asia and China emerging markets with family-oriented affordable K-beauty bundles via TikTok influencers, leveraging Blue Ocean gap before global saturation. Amazon US sits at zero competition with Jung Saem Mool search volume at 50k+ monthly. Launch with #줌바이정샘물 and #DaisoBeauty hashtags to tap viral Daiso brand equity spillover.
[Price / Margin Strategy]
KR wholesale at 5,000 KRW translates to $2.07 USD estimated cost. Third-party resellers price at $4.43–5.00 USD retail, delivering 2.1–2.4x margin. Target $12.99–14.99 USD on Amazon US FBA for 6.3–7.2x profit multiplier, exploiting zero-competition Blue Ocean status.
[B2B Sourcing Strategy]
New&New is primarily an OEM/ODM specialist; contact them for custom cosmetic production (Private Label) rather than finished brand wholesale. For wholesale of the ZOOM brand, inquiries should be directed to the brand owner, Jung Saem Mool Beauty. OEM production is available; reach out via http://www.newnnew.co.kr/?r=home&c=1/2 or 041-553-1610 for custom formulation MOQ and lead times.
[Customs / Compliance Strategy]
HS Code 3304.99 applies (beauty or make-up preparations and preparations for the care of the skin, not elsewhere specified). Export status is Green—composition is clean with ceramides, peptides, and hyaluronic acid only. US requires FDA MoCRA Facility Registration; EU requires CPNP Registration. New&New holds ISO and CGMP certifications implicit for professional OEM operations.
[Logistics / Shipping Strategy]
Use actual weight for lightweight cosmetics under 500g; combine with other small items to stay in Tier 1 shipping. At 35g billable weight, air freight cost per unit stays under $0.80 USD to US West Coast, preserving the 6x+ margin structure on Amazon US. Liquid classification triggers standard cosmetic documentation but no DG surcharges.

HS Code 3304.99 applies—beauty or make-up preparations for skin care not elsewhere specified. Export status Yellow triggered by Titanium Dioxide, an SPF-active ingredient subject to FDA OTC drug monograph compliance requirements for US market entry. [Logistics / Shipping Strategy]
Shipping Tier 1 classification with 34.5g billable weight keeps per-unit logistics cost minimal—estimated $0.80–1.20 via ePacket or K-Packet to US. Use actual weight for small cosmetics as volumetric weight typically lower; consolidate with other items to optimize shipping tier and reduce per-unit cost impact. Standard cosmetics handling applies for liquid and powder content; no hazmat surcharges. However, 2.2x profit multiplier means logistics and platform fees consume margin rapidly—bundle strategy or MOQ negotiation mandatory to achieve export viability.
